entries.tutorialSubtitle: Why the 6–9 mm gap controls everything in a managed hive, and how to keep your equipment working with it.
entries.tutorialExcerpt: The discovery that bees leave a 6 to 9 mm gap clear inside a managed hive (enough to pass through but not enough to fill with comb or propolis) changed beekeeping. Lorenzo Langstroth published the principle in 1851. Every movable-frame hive design since has been built around it.
